speaker: Guest
text_en: Yeah, that's a good point. While you're being practical and helpful, it made me think other practical helpful thoughts. So thank you for inspiring real advice. There is going to be a gap in your current team with you spreading your attention over two teams now. And one way you could do this is some delegation of, I don't know if you have a team lead that isn't you on this team,
text: 对，这个点很好。你刚才讲得很务实，也很有帮助，这也让我想到了一些别的务实建议。所以谢谢你启发出了真正有用的建议。你现在要把注意力分到两个团队上，那你当前团队里一定会出现空缺。有一种做法是做一些授权，我不知道你这个团队里除了你之外有没有 team lead，

speaker: Guest
text_en: but maybe you probably don't have authority to do like a battlefield promotion and say great. One person on each team is now an EM and they both report to me and probably can't do that, but you can arrange some of your responsibilities to be delegated to people on your old team and give them a little bit more direct support in somebody else on the team that you assign some stuff to.
text: 不过你大概率没有权限搞什么战场晋升，直接说很好，现在每个团队各有一个人升成 EM，都向我汇报——这大概做不到。但你可以把自己原来团队里的一些职责委派出去，交给团队中的一些人，并且给被你分配到这些事情的人更多一点直接支持。

speaker: Guest
text_en: It is different. It's different than you being there full time as your only team, but that is another option besides just like drop some stuff and spread your effort among twice as many people now.
text: 这当然不一样，和你全职只带这一个团队肯定不同，但除了单纯地砍掉一些事情、然后把自己的精力摊到两倍的人身上之外，这也是另一个选择。

speaker: Host
text_en: Yeah, and that's the creative approach, I think, as well as like try to come up with new ways of doing things that are a little different. text: 对，我觉得这也是一种有创意的做法，就是试着想出一些新的、稍微不一样的做事方式。你知道，也许没法像以前那样做到那么有人情味，你得做一些取舍，但也许你可以用更有创意的方式来处理这个情况，照样把它变成一个很不错的局面。
